question: Which allegations have no supporting evidence?
reply:
There are **three pleaded allegations currently carrying a MISSING evidential status**, meaning no supporting evidence has been logged against them in the proof matrix:

---

### 1. [P0005] — Negligence
> *"The Defendant's conduct constituted negligence."*

This is a freestanding tort claim. Without expert or documentary evidence establishing a duty of care, breach and causation, this head of claim is currently unsupported and vulnerable to strike-out or summary judgment.

---

### 2. [P0006] — Misrepresentation
> *"The Defendant's conduct constituted misrepresentation."*

This is the legal conclusion underpinning the misrepresentation cause of action. It links directly to [P0016] (the alleged oral representation by Mr Frost) and [P0018] below — both of which also have evidential problems.

---

### 3. [P0018] — Falsity of the Representation
> *"The Representation was false: the Platform was incapable of supporting a volume of at least 10,000 concurrent transactions."*

This is the factual cornerstone of the misrepresentation claim. Without technical evidence (e.g. load-testing data, expert report or internal TechFlow communications) demonstrating the Platform could not handle 10,000 concurrent transactions, the misrepresentation claim at [P0006] cannot stand.

---

### Practical note
All three MISSING propositions are interconnected — [P0018] feeds [P0006], and [P0006] underpins [P0005]. Securing a technical expert report on platform capacity would likely address [P0018] and give traction to both [P0005] and [P0006] simultaneously. These should be treated as **urgent evidence-gathering priorities** given trial readiness is currently at **59%**.
